results.possibleIdentity: Harkishandas V. Desai (active in the late 1960s and early 1970s) was a mechanical engineer and designer associated with the National Institute of Design (NID) in Ahmedabad, India. During his tenure at NID, he worked on industrial design projects, most notably the development of stainless steel flatware (cutlery) for Nagpal Metal Industries, Bombay. This project involved comprehensive ergonomic studies to determine final product shapes for export markets. His work is documented in NID's institutional records from 1964–1969. Desai was part of the professional network associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and others to facilitate collaborations between artists and engineers. His inclusion in E.A.T.'s technical service records (index cards) reflects NID's status as a hub for such interdisciplinary collaborations in India during the late 1960s, a period when Klüver visited the institute to engage with Indian engineers and scientists.
